title: Multi-tenant analytics workbench factory-of-factories
problem: A tightly coupled ai-teams workflow serializes independent complex-application branches through one growing conversation, reducing throughput and increasing context drag.
objective: Build a multi-tenant analytics workbench by dispatching independent AO Operator branches through AO orchestration.
success_criteria: The generated RunSpec contains the full 17-task factory-of-factories topology.
Spec Forge and Ralph Loop gates run before factory-manager dispatch.
Five implementation factory branches and five reviewer branches are independently materialized with scoped prompts.
Validation proves the topology, prompt set, and contract fields are complete.
constraints: Keep branch context bounded to scoped reads, writes, and durable artifacts.
Fan out only after Spec Forge and Ralph Loop pass.
sensitive_fields: tenant data samples that contain private identifiers
negative_constraints: MUST NOT configure OPENAI_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
MUST NOT pass full transcripts between branches
MUST NOT dispatch branches with overlapping writes unless the integrator owns the merge contract
MUST NOT accept closure when any branch returns BLOCKED or REJECTED

shalls.text: The factory plan SHALL declare disjoint read and write scopes for contract, backend, frontend, quality, and docs branches.
AO orchestration SHALL dispatch the five implementation factories in parallel after Spec Forge and Ralph Loop gates pass.
Every branch SHALL return a STATUS block with Result, Artifact, Evidence, Concerns, and Blocker fields.
Evaluator closure SHALL fan in reviewed artifacts and AO event evidence instead of full provider transcripts.

acceptance_criteria.description: The topology contains planner, Spec Forge, Ralph Loop, plan-hardener, factory-manager, five parallel factories, five parallel reviewers, integrator, and evaluator.
acceptance_criteria.oracle: Topology and RunSpec task checks pass.

acceptance_criteria.description: Provider selection uses OAuth CLI providers only and resolves Codex for implementation branches and Claude for review/closure gates.
acceptance_criteria.oracle: Factory doctor reports valid provider resolution and forbidden API key env vars are absent.

acceptance_criteria.description: The critical path model demonstrates fan-out/fan-in instead of serialized implementation and review turns.
acceptance_criteria.oracle: Expected-throughput document names the fan-out critical path and measurable branch checks.

verification: acceptance fixtures are referenced by backend and frontend branches
tenant isolation and ingestion validation are covered
e2e, security, and performance harness commands are documented
setup guide and prompt samples cover AO Operator operation

trigger_hints: security-reviewer for tenant isolation and auth boundaries
performance-reviewer for ingestion and dashboard budget
docs-writer for setup and prompt samples
